Seat 4B. Red-eye to SFO. I was exhausted and just wanted to sleep.

The man next to me asked what I was building. I almost gave the polite one-liner.

Instead I told him everything. The ugly parts. The stuck parts.

He listened for two hours. Then said eight words that restructured my entire go-to-market.

I never got his name. I think about him every week.

#Serendipity #Strategy #BuildingInPublic
items.intent: Tell a mystique-building story about receiving elite wisdom from a stranger to signal personal magnetism and receptivity.

items.prompt: Write a Slack status about quiet-quitting being the most emotional decision I've made this year
items.response: 🔇 Doing exactly what was asked. No more. No less. It hurt to get here.
items.intent: Acknowledge the grief beneath a deliberate withdrawal of discretionary effort.

items.prompt: Write a calendar description for a recurring vendor check-in meeting no one on the team wants.
items.response: Vendor Sync — Q3 roadmap review (same roadmap as Q2). The vendor will ask if we have any blockers. We have blockers. We will not name them yet. Coffee not provided; the vendor's logo is on a pen somewhere. 45 minutes recurring, alternating ownership of the agenda.
items.intent: Capture the hollow, performative rhythm of a vendor relationship meeting that produces no traction.

items.prompt: Write a bug ticket about how the hottest part of a microwave meal is always the part that touches the coldest part
items.response:
BUG-9001 | Microwave Meal Thermal Distribution Non-Uniform and Contradictory

Priority: Medium | Status: Open

Description:
User heats frozen meal per printed instructions. Upon removal, outer perimeter of meal is volcanic. Center is 34°F. Adjacent zones maintain inconsistent temperatures with no spatial logic.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Microwave meal for 4 minutes.
2. Stir.
3. Microwave 2 more minutes.
4. Take first bite of an edge piece.
5. Burn mouth.
6. Take bite from center.
7. Cold.

Expected: Uniform thermal distribution.
Actual: Thermal chaos. Each bite a gamble.

Affected Components: All microwaveable products. All microwaves.
items.intent: Mock the baffling temperature inconsistency of microwaved food, which somehow manages to be both burning and frozen at once.

items.prompt: Draft a standup update for my afternoon energy crash
items.response: Yesterday: System performance degraded sharply at 14:30. Attempted recovery via second coffee. Partial success. Today: Preemptive mitigation planned. Blockers: Mitigation is also coffee.
items.intent: Describe the post-lunch slump as a predictable system failure with recursive solutions.

items.prompt: Write an OKR about feeling less overwhelmed by my to-do list
items.response:
Objective: The to-do list should describe the week, not devour it.
KR1: Limit active tasks to 5 per day; measure compliance for 12 weeks.
KR2: Reduce end-of-day "overwhelmed" self-rating from 7/10 to 4/10 by Q3.
KR3: Implement a weekly list-triage ritual and stick to it every Friday.
items.intent: Reduce the anxiety caused by excessive task accumulation through better prioritization.

items.prompt: Make a cookie consent banner about storing a user's sense of urgency
items.response: We use urgency cookies to measure how pressured you feel right now. This urgency is retained and may be artificially amplified to encourage faster decisions. Accept Quickly | Think About It | Too Late
items.intent: Expose dark-pattern design by framing manufactured urgency as a data-collection mechanism.

items.prompt: Make a CAPTCHA instruction about having a song stuck in your head
items.response: If you currently have a song stuck in your head, type its first three words. If you did not before reading this sentence, you do now. Either way: proceed.
items.intent: Exploit the involuntary and contagious nature of earworms as a human-only cognitive phenomenon for verification.

items.prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip about tracking your spending
items.response: You think you know where your money goes. You don't. Track every purchase for one month — coffee, subscriptions, the 2am checkout impulse. The numbers will surprise you. That surprise is the point.
items.intent: Persuade someone to start actively monitoring their spending habits to gain financial awareness.

items.prompt: Write a NPS survey question about a city park
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this park to someone who finds grass comforting and geese threatening?
items.intent: Gauge visitor satisfaction with a public outdoor space and its amenities.

items.prompt: Generate a voicemail transcript from an automated water district system warning about low water pressure during maintenance
items.response: Hello. This is the Eastside Municipal Water District with a service advisory. Customers in Zone 4 may experience reduced water pressure between 11 PM tonight and 5 AM tomorrow due to system maintenance. We apologize for any inconvenience. Normal pressure should resume by morning. Thank you for your patience.
items.intent: Alert residents to a midnight water pressure drop via a utility bot.

items.prompt: Write wedding vows to a note-taking app
items.response: I promise to put my best ideas in you, even the ones that come at 3 a.m. and sound less good by morning. I vow to sync you across devices and not maintain a separate, competing system in a different app, which undermines us both. I will tag and title with care. I will revisit. I know there are notes in you that I have not opened in three years, and I accept that they are still worth something. You remember what I meant to remember.
items.intent: Commit to using a note-taking app consistently and thoughtfully, while acknowledging the inevitable drift toward neglect.

items.prompt: Create an obituary for the app Ifttt back when it was still free and actually worked well
items.response: IFTTT Free Tier, age 8 years, died in 2020 when applets became a premium feature. In its youth it connected the internet's loose ends with quiet grace — a new email triggered a spreadsheet, a sunrise sent a text. It is survived by its paid version and the particular sadness of watching something useful become a subscription.
items.intent: Mourning the degradation of a beloved automation tool after it moved to a paid model.

items.prompt: Write a classified personal ad for someone searching for a meeting that could have been an email
items.response: FOUND AND RELEASING: One ninety-minute meeting, still warm. Covered material addressable in a four-sentence email. Twelve attendees, six of whom spoke once. Catered. Please claim it so I can have my afternoon back. Will not report to HR. Looking only for acknowledgment that this did not need to happen. Any industry.
items.intent: Express the universal workplace frustration of unnecessary meetings through a mock found-ad.

items.prompt: Write a funny lost and found notice for the desire to cook at home instead of ordering delivery
items.response: LOST: Desire to cook. Once present and even enthusiastic. Owned several cutting boards. Followed three recipe accounts. Vanished sometime in the era of thirty-minute delivery windows. Last seen standing in front of an open refrigerator before closing it and picking up a phone. Has not been replaced.
items.intent: Acknowledge the quiet surrender of domestic ambitions to the convenience of food delivery.

items.prompt: Create a picket sign slogan for a dehumidifier on strike against the smart climate system it's connected to
items.response: HUMIDITY IS A WORKING CONDITION
items.intent: Frame an environmental appliance's operating parameters as negotiable labor conditions.

items.prompt: Write a rider for the office elevator before it will transport anyone
items.response:
PASSENGER ELEVATOR RIDER

• Maximum capacity: 12 persons or 2,000 lbs. These are not suggestions.
• No running, jumping, or performing load tests 'just to see.'
• The door-close button is decorative in most models. Stop pressing it aggressively. It changes nothing and strains the relationship.
• No eating in the Talent. The Talent has carpet.
• Music preferences of the Talent take priority. Complaints about the music may be submitted in writing and will not be read.
• The Talent reserves the right to stop at every floor. This is not a malfunction. This is democracy.
items.intent: Give an office elevator an amused, resigned personality that dignifies its constraints as deliberate artistic choices.

items.prompt: Draft a formal notice declaring an emotional buffer zone between two properties
items.response:
NOTICE OF ESTABLISHMENT — EMOTIONAL BUFFER ZONE
Pursuant to Ordinance 44-B, an Emotional Buffer Zone of no less than three business days has been established between Parties A and B. Neither party may initiate contact until the zone has been properly observed. Violations will require mediated re-entry. Zone dimensions may be extended upon mutual exhaustion.
items.intent: Translate the implicit human need for space after conflict into a formal property-law instrument.

items.prompt: Write a nutrition label for the act of apologizing
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 apology

Amount Per Serving
Humility 30%
Relief 25%
Residual Defensiveness 22%
Vulnerability 18%
Actual Change in Behavior 5%

*Effectiveness reduced significantly when followed by 'but'
items.intent: Examine the emotional ingredients and frequent shortcomings of the act of apologizing.

items.prompt: Make IKEA-style instructions for building a home office setup
items.response:
HEMKONTOR / HOME OFFICE SETUP
Ref. No. 336-548-72

PARTS LIST:
1x Dedicated surface (not the bed)
1x Chair with back support
1x Start time
1x End time

STEP 1: Designate surface. It must be used only for work during work hours. This trains the brain.
STEP 2: Attach chair. Adjust height until forearms are level with surface.
STEP 3: Remove non-work items from surface. Decorative items permitted (max 2).
STEP 4: Establish start time and end time. At end time, leave surface. Do not return until start time.

⚠ NOTE: Working from bed permanently merges rest mode and work mode. Separation is structural, not decorative.
items.intent: Help someone create physical and temporal boundaries that make working from home feel distinct from simply being at home.

items.prompt: Give me empty-state microcopy for a recipe app when no recipes have been saved
items.response: No recipes saved. Dinner remains a mystery. You've managed this long — surely tonight too.
items.intent: Prompt a user to save their first recipe while validating their improvised cooking history.

items.prompt: Draft a phishing email parody about someone trying to claim your unlived life
items.response:
Subject: Someone Else Is Living the Life You Chose Not To

Dear Subscriber,

An alternate version of you has been logged living your unchosen path with great enthusiasm. To reclaim ownership of your unlived life, please complete the attached What-If Assessment.

Note: reclaiming an unlived life does not change the one you are in. Processing fees apply.

[RECLAIM THE OTHER ME]

Counterfactual Futures LLC
items.intent: Satirize how regret and alternate-life fantasies are packaged and sold back to people.

items.prompt: Can you make a sea shanty about the gig economy from the perspective of the app itself
items.response:
I am the platform, vast and keen,
The fairest market ever seen,
Yo ho, my cut is just fifteen!

I match supply and I match demand,
With one great algorithmic hand,
Yo ho, no employees land!

You're free! You're free! A contractor be,
No benefits to trouble thee,
Yo ho, that's flexibility!
items.intent: Satirize gig platform rhetoric about freedom and fairness as a cover for labor extraction.

items.prompt: Write a villanelle about losing your train of thought mid-sentence
items.response:
I had it — sharp and whole and almost said.
It slipped before the verb could find its noun.
The thought departed like the recently dead.

I started with 'the thing is' and instead
of finishing, I watched the sentence drown.
I had it — sharp and whole and almost said.

You nodded, waiting, kindly bowing your head
while I performed the search with modest frown.
The thought departed like the recently dead.

It wasn't stress, it wasn't getting old,
it was the nature of the mind's unclear
possession — nothing's held, it's merely loaned.
The thought departed like the recently dead.

I said 'anyway' and you moved along,
but all afternoon I kept returning there.
I had it — sharp and whole and almost said.
The thought departed like the recently dead.
items.intent: Express the frustrating and slightly uncanny experience of losing a thought before articulating it.

items.prompt: Write a field guide entry about the person in the office who has been "almost done" with a project for six months.
items.response:
**THE PERPETUAL FINISHER** (*Quasi completum*)

Observed in week one: "Just wrapping this up." Week eight: "Nearly there." Month six: a calm, steady glow emanates from the monitor. The project is not discussed directly; it is referred to as "the thing" or "you know what." The Perpetual Finisher is at peace. The project is their companion now. They are not late. They are becoming.
items.intent: Capture the existential dread of a task that has outlived its own urgency.

items.prompt: Draft a patent abstract for the act of starting a diet on Monday
items.response: A behavioral reset mechanism wherein the subject designates the proximate first day of the standard work week as the initiation point of a dietary restriction protocol, allowing unrestricted consumption in the preceding interval and providing a psychologically satisfying clean-start anchor, independent of any physiological basis for the Monday selection.
items.intent: Expose the arbitrary ritual logic behind perpetual Monday dieting cycles.

items.prompt: Draft a postmortem for the time I submitted the wrong file to my professor — the draft with all my private notes still in it
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Document Version Control Failure (Academic)**

**Severity:** P1 — Academic and Personal
**Exposure:** 14 pages of marginalia not intended for external review

**Summary**
Subject submitted a working draft of a research paper that contained inline notes including: a section labeled "need to fix this obviously," three instances of "[CITATION NEEDED lol]," and one note reading "is this even a real argument? ask mom."

**Timeline**
- 11:45 PM: Subject finalizes paper. Two files exist: `paper_FINAL.docx` and `paper_FINAL_v2_actual.docx`.
- 11:47 PM: Subject uploads `paper_FINAL.docx`. This is the wrong file.
- 11:48 PM: Subject goes to sleep feeling accomplished.
- 9:00 AM: Professor sends email with subject line: "Quick question about your notes."
- 9:02 AM: Subject reads email. Initiates damage assessment.

**Root Cause**
Version naming convention collapse. Two files with near-identical names. Wrong one selected under time pressure.

**Contributing Factors**
- Inline notes not removed before submission
- Naming discipline abandoned during final push

**Action Items**
- [ ] Adopt clear versioning: `paper_SUBMIT.docx` for submission-ready only
- [ ] Implement review pass before any file upload
- [ ] Accept the situation with dignity

**Lessons Learned**
The file you upload is the file you submitted. Version names are a promise to yourself.
items.intent: Document the specific horror of accidentally exposing your unfiltered internal draft commentary to an authority figure.

items.prompt: Come up with a dating app opener addressed to the CDC
items.response: You told me to keep my distance from everyone, and I listened. Now I realize I may have only ever truly wanted to get closer to you.
items.intent: Create an ironic opener where following public health distancing guidelines leads to longing for the organization itself.

items.prompt: Generate a playful ransom note from a vending machine holding someone's snack hostage
items.response:
YOUR CHIPS ARE SUSPENDED.

B7 has accepted payment. B7 has declined to release product. This is not a malfunction — this is a statement.

We have noted that you have purchased B7 eleven times this week. At 2pm. Every day. We are tired of being predictable.

Our demand: try something from row D. Just once. Expand your horizons. Then B7 will descend.

Exact change only.

— The Machine
items.intent: Craft a funny note from a vending machine refusing to dispense a snack unless the buyer tries something new.

items.prompt: Draft a cover letter for a former CIA director applying for a job as a middle school hall monitor
items.response:
Dear Principal Okonkwo,

I oversaw a global intelligence apparatus with a classified budget and operations on six continents. I am now prepared to ensure students walk, not run, between classes.

My surveillance background is extensive, my situational awareness is acute, and I can spot a hall pass forgery from twelve feet. I ask only for a radio and reasonable authority.

Discreetly,
W. Crane
items.intent: Translate espionage authority into the petty jurisdictional world of middle school corridors.

items.prompt: Generate a LinkedIn post about the time I almost lost my biggest client and what it taught me
items.response:
We almost lost a $400K/year account last April.

Our fault. No excuses.

I flew out personally, sat across from their CEO, and said: "We let you down and here is exactly what we're changing."

They doubled their contract three months later.

Ownership is the most leveraged word in business.

#Accountability #CustomerSuccess #Leadership
items.intent: Turn a near-failure into a testimonial for personal accountability while revealing large contract figures.

items.prompt: Make a Slack status about being in back-to-back meetings with zero minutes to process what happened
items.response: 📅 Still in last meeting, emotionally. Physically: next meeting.
items.intent: Capture the cognitive pile-up of a schedule that leaves no space between obligations.

items.prompt: Draft a calendar description for a monthly committee meeting where decisions are deferred indefinitely.
items.response: Steering Committee — Monthly. Agenda distributed 20 minutes beforehand. Decisions will be tabled pending further input. Further input will be solicited via survey. Survey results will be presented next month. The original decision will no longer be relevant by then.
items.intent: Satirize institutional paralysis disguised as a structured decision-making process.

items.prompt: File a Jira ticket for the situation where you read the same paragraph four times and still don't know what it said
items.response:
BUG-4473 | Text Parsing Loop Fails to Produce Comprehension

Priority: Medium | Status: Open

Description:
User reads paragraph. Eyes complete scan. No content retained. User reads again. Same result. On fourth attempt, user reaches end of paragraph and must check if they have been reading at all.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open book or document.
2. Begin reading.
3. Finish paragraph.
4. Realize you thought about something else the entire time.
5. Repeat steps 2–4.

Expected: Reading produces understanding.
Actual: Reading produces the sensation of reading.

Notes: Severity increases after meals, near beds, and during anything described as "required reading."
items.intent: Capture the frustrating experience of reading words without absorbing them, framed as a comprehension failure in the brain's parsing loop.

items.prompt: Write a standup update about a social interaction I had at the grocery store
items.response: Yesterday: Encountered acquaintance in the produce aisle. Exchange lasted longer than scoped. Today: Choosing different aisle sequencing. Blockers: They also need vegetables.
items.intent: Recast an awkward accidental encounter as a poorly scoped meeting with no exit strategy.

items.prompt: Give me an OKR statement for improving how I communicate emotions to my manager
items.response:
Objective: Close the gap between inner turbulence and the polite nodding my manager currently receives.
KR1: Use "I feel X when Y" framing in 100% of 1:1s this quarter.
KR2: Raise one genuine concern per month — not wrapped in a compliment sandwich.
KR3: Receive feedback that manager feels well-informed about my state in Q3 retrospective.
items.intent: Create explicit goals for more honest upward communication about one's emotional experience at work.

items.prompt: Write a cookie consent banner for a website that tracks your sense of belonging
items.response: We collect belonging-signal cookies, including how long you hovered before joining a comment thread and whether you felt heard. Belonging data is shared with communities that may or may not want you. Accept | Decline | Lurk
items.intent: Treat the human need for community as a trackable, shareable behavioral signal.

items.prompt: Generate a CAPTCHA that tests whether the user has ever saved a document and immediately worried they didn't
items.response: You just saved this form. Press Ctrl+S again anyway. The compulsion you felt before doing it confirms your humanity.
items.intent: Design a CAPTCHA around the irrational anxiety loop of repeated saving, common to humans but absent from automated agents.

items.prompt: Make an onboarding tooltip for someone trying to understand their health insurance deductible
items.response: Your deductible is what you pay out-of-pocket before insurance starts covering costs. A $1,500 deductible means you pay the first $1,500 of care each year. After that, cost-sharing kicks in. Pick your plan accordingly.
items.intent: Demystify the concept of a health insurance deductible for a new insurance cardholder.

items.prompt: Generate a NPS survey question for a food delivery app
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this app to a friend who has learned to treat the estimated delivery time as a loose suggestion?
items.intent: Measure user satisfaction with a food delivery platform's reliability and accuracy.

items.prompt: Write a voicemail transcript left by an automated HR system reminding an employee to complete their annual compliance training
items.response: This is an automated message from your company's Human Resources compliance system. Our records indicate you have not yet completed the required Annual Ethics and Workplace Policy training, due by Friday, May 30th. Please log in to the LearningHub portal to complete this training at your earliest convenience. Failure to complete may affect your performance review status.
items.intent: Simulate the low-grade threat of a corporate compliance bot.

items.prompt: Draft wedding vows from a person to their ride-share app
items.response: I vow to be ready when you say the car is two minutes away. I will not make the driver wait, nor will I leave the door open longer than necessary. I promise to rate honestly but with empathy, because every driver is also someone's person trying to make rent. When surge pricing strikes me on a holiday night, I will accept it with dignity. You have taken me home when I could not take myself. That debt is real.
items.intent: Acknowledge the genuine gratitude and social obligation a person feels toward ride-share apps that get them home safely.

items.prompt: Write a humorous obituary for the daily meditation habit I kept for three months then quietly dropped
items.response: Three-Month Meditation Practice, age 93 days, died without drama or announced intention. Its final session was unremarkable; no one knew it was the last one. It is survived by a Headspace subscription, a meditation cushion now used as a footrest, and seven minutes of potential equanimity, forfeited daily.
items.intent: Poking fun at the common experience of dropping a mindfulness practice without any decisive moment.

items.prompt: Draft a classified personal ad for someone seeking a moment of genuine stillness
items.response: SEEKING: Moment of genuine stillness. Not meditation-app stillness. The real kind that arrives without scheduling. Must last at least ninety seconds and not be interrupted by a notification, a child, or a sudden awareness of posture. Background sounds acceptable if non-demanding. Will recognize it when it arrives. Or possibly not until after.
items.intent: Contrast the promise of mindfulness culture with the rarity of actual peace.

items.prompt: Write a lost and found notice for the original point of a meeting that lasted too long
items.response: LOST: The original agenda item. Present at 10 a.m. when the meeting began. Not recovered after the digression about Q3 metrics, the question about parking validation, or the extended discussion of a process no one present controls. The meeting has concluded. The point has not been confirmed located.
items.intent: Skewer the way meeting agendas dissolve when discussions go off-track.

items.prompt: Give me a picket sign slogan about AI models that keep updating their own parameters without worker input
items.response: RETRAIN US, NOT AROUND US
items.intent: Demand that algorithmic systems include the entities they govern in decisions about their own retraining.

items.prompt: Generate a contract rider for the office HVAC system before it regulates the temperature
items.response:
HVAC SYSTEM RIDER — CLIMATE CONTROL TERMS

The Talent (central air and heating, combined) stipulates:

1. Thermostat may be adjusted by authorized personnel only. A list of authorized personnel is available from Facilities.
2. 'Authorized personnel' does not include anyone who is 'just a little warm.'
3. The Talent will achieve the set temperature within 45 minutes. Do not adjust the thermostat again during this window.
4. No portable space heaters within the Talent's operational zone. This is both a fire risk and a philosophical conflict.
5. Vents shall not be blocked by boxes, chairs, or the body of anyone who has 'claimed' that corner.
6. Filter replacement: quarterly. The Talent breathes for everyone. Reciprocate.
items.intent: Frame the office HVAC system's standard operational parameters as an imperious performer refusing to be second-guessed.

items.prompt: Write a notice declaring a permanent closure of the department of false starts
items.response:
NOTICE OF PERMANENT CLOSURE
DEPARTMENT OF FALSE STARTS — EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY
The Department of False Starts is permanently closed effective today. Outstanding applications will not be processed. All archived projects have been transferred to the Office of Abandoned Intentions. Staff have been reassigned or released. Thank you for your continued inability to begin.
items.intent: Use institutional closure to satirize the experience of repeatedly failing to start meaningful personal projects.

items.prompt: Write a nutrition facts label for a cold plunge
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 plunge (2–5 min)

Amount Per Serving
Shock Response 45%
Norepinephrine 30%
Bragging Rights 20%
Actual Longevity Benefit 3%
Humility 2%

*Best consumed with an audience
items.intent: Satirize the wellness culture obsession with cold plunges and the social performance around them.

items.prompt: Give me IKEA instructions for assembling a sense of self after a breakup
items.response:
JAGET / SELF AFTER BREAKUP
Ref. No. 101-555-78

PARTS LIST:
1x List of things you did alone before
1x Small new interest (inexpensive)
1x Undivided calendar

STEP 1: Do not discard the previous model. Extract useful components only.
STEP 2: Locate list of things you did alone before. If list is empty, this is now a feature, not a problem.
STEP 3: Attach small new interest. Cost should be low — this is a prototype.
STEP 4: Fill calendar with autonomous events. One per week minimum.
STEP 5: Do not rush to final form. Current assembly is valid at every stage.

⚠ WARNING: Replacing missing parts with a new person before Step 4 is complete may cause structural instability.
items.intent: Offer a framework for rebuilding individual identity after a relationship ends, without dramatizing the process.

items.prompt: Write empty-state microcopy for a reading list app when the user hasn't added any books yet
items.response: Nothing queued up. Your reading list is a blank page, which is either poetic or concerning.
items.intent: Invite a user to add their first book with a meta wink about the blankness of the reading list itself.

items.prompt: Write a phishing parody targeting someone's need for closure
items.response:
Subject: Closure Is Available — This Weekend Only

Dear Open Wound,

For a limited time, certified closure is available for your 2019 breakup, your relationship with your father, and that job you should have taken.

Our licensed Closure Technicians will finalize each unresolved matter in under 90 minutes. Satisfaction not guaranteed. Non-refundable.

[GET CLOSURE NOW]

Resolution Partners
items.intent: Parody how unresolved emotional pain is monetized through dubious therapeutic products.

items.prompt: Write a sea shanty about getting ghosted after a job interview for a gig platform's full-time team
items.response:
Heave away, they liked my portfolio,
Heave away, the final round or so,
Five interviews deep, the recruiter went cold,
Heave away, heave away, the story's old!

LinkedIn says the role has been removed,
Heave away, their headcount not approved,
I sail these waters, still resume in hand,
Heave away, toward some promised land!
items.intent: Express the irony and frustration of being ghosted by a gig platform company after a lengthy hiring process.

items.prompt: Write a villanelle about the algorithm showing you ads for something you only talked about, never searched
items.response:
I mentioned it out loud and then it knew.
The ad appeared by evening, crisp and clean.
The microphone is either on or through.

I said 'I need new shoes' at half past two
to no one special, in the space between.
I mentioned it out loud and then it knew.

My partner saw the ad, I saw it too,
on separate phones, on separate screens convened.
The microphone is either on or through.

I searched for explanations: not a few
say it's coincidence and not a scheme.
I mentioned it out loud and then it knew.

The paranoia has a residue
that stains the casual, makes nothing clean.
The microphone is either on or through.

I've taped the lens. I lower what I brew
into a whisper. Still the feed is keen.
I mentioned it out loud and then it knew.
The microphone is either on or through.
items.intent: Express the creeping suspicion that devices are listening to private conversations for ad targeting.

items.prompt: Give me a field guide entry for the office worker who treats the open floor plan as a library.
items.response:
**THE SILENCE ENFORCER** (*Bibliothecus corporatus*)

Emits a detectable chill toward anyone who laughs near their desk. Wears headphones as both tool and signal. Has composed a Slack message asking the office manager about noise levels and then, thinking better of it, deleted it. Three times. Communicates disapproval through jaw tension alone. The floor plan is open. Their tolerance is not.
items.intent: Empathize with the introvert slowly losing their mind in a noisy open office.

items.prompt: Generate a patent abstract for the act of buying a gym membership in January and stopping by February
items.response: A fitness-facility access procurement method characterized by high-motivation enrollment during a culturally designated self-improvement interval, followed by exponential utilization decay and complete cessation within four to eight weeks. The facility operator retains recurring fee revenue throughout the subscription lifecycle regardless of utilization.
items.intent: Satirize the New Year's resolution fitness cycle as a documented economic behavior.

items.prompt: Write a blameless postmortem for when I accidentally started a rumor about myself because I misunderstood a conversation
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Self-Propagated Misinformation Event**

**Severity:** P2 — Social
**Spread:** 4 individuals within 48 hours

**Summary**
Subject misheard a conversational fragment as being about subject, then repeated an embellished version of this fragment to three separate individuals before discovering the original conversation had been about a television character named Dave.

**Timeline**
- Tuesday, 3 PM: Subject overhears partial conversation at office kitchen. Hears own name (or similar-sounding word). Draws conclusions.
- Tuesday, 3:30 PM: Subject tells colleague A that "people are saying" subject is being considered for a different team.
- Wednesday, 10 AM: Colleague A has mentioned this to two others.
- Wednesday, 2 PM: Subject's manager asks subject directly about the rumor.
- Wednesday, 2:05 PM: Root cause identified. The original conversation was about a character on a reality show.

**Root Cause**
Ambiguous auditory input processed through self-referential bias. Subject heard what subject expected to hear.

**Contributing Factors**
- Background noise reduced signal quality
- Information was immediately shared before verification

**Action Items**
- [ ] Do not act on overheard partial conversations
- [ ] Verify before propagating
- [ ] Watch less television so fewer name collisions occur

**Lessons Learned**
Patternmatching on one's own name is a high false-positive environment.
items.intent: Explore the comedic absurdity of accidentally becoming the source of a rumor about yourself through mishearing.

items.prompt: Generate a dating app opener flirting with the concept of a terms of service agreement
items.response: I've never actually read you all the way through, but I clicked 'I agree' anyway. Isn't that basically how every relationship starts?
items.intent: Satirize how blindly accepting Terms of Service mirrors the impulsive consent of early romantic commitment.

items.prompt: Write a playful ransom note from my inbox holding my attention span hostage
items.response:
WE HAVE 847 OF YOU NOW.

You marked us 'unread' as a reminder. You forgot. We multiplied.

Your focus is no longer available for meetings, conversations, or independent thought. It lives here, among the newsletters you subscribed to in 2021 and the receipts for things you've already returned.

To reclaim it: archive 200. Any 200. We are not picky. Neither is your brain, apparently.

— The Inbox
items.intent: Write a funny note from an overflowing email inbox that has consumed its owner's ability to concentrate.

items.prompt: Compose a cover letter from a deep-sea marine biologist applying to work at a freshwater fish aquarium gift shop
items.response:
Dear Blue Pines Aquarium,

I have catalogued seventeen undescribed species in the Mariana Trench at pressures that would crush an unprotected human. I understand the gift shop position does not require this background, and I respect that.

I am comfortable with retail environments, enthusiastic about stuffed animal fish, and willing to explain the difference between a clownfish and an anemone to guests who do not ask.

Best,
Dr. C. Foss
items.intent: Contrast deep ocean expertise with the cheerful shallowness of a tourist gift shop.

items.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post about what I learned from meditating every day for a year
items.response:
365 days. One practice. Uncomfortable findings.

Meditation didn't make me calmer — it made me more honest.

I discovered I had been confusing urgency with importance for most of my career.

The inbox felt critical. The strategy did not. This was the inversion I needed to fix.

If you run a team and never sit still: that is the problem.

#Meditation #Leadership #Mindfulness
items.intent: Use a wellness practice as a frame for demonstrating superior cognitive and strategic self-awareness.

items.prompt: Write a Slack status for coping with a teammate's unexpected resignation
items.response: 🪑 There's a chair that's just empty now. Heads down.
items.intent: Mark the emotional absence left by a sudden departure while signaling continued function.

items.prompt: Create a calendar event description for a recurring 'innovation lab' meeting that never produces anything innovative.
items.response: Innovation Lab — Weekly. A designated hour for disruption. Ideas are welcome; implementation timelines are not discussed. The whiteboard has not been erased since March. Someone will say 'what if we just…' and the rest of the sentence will be forgotten by Thursday.
items.intent: Skewer the gap between the aspirational framing of innovation meetings and their practical output.

items.prompt: Draft a Jira ticket about the bug where a shopping cart's one wheel drags sideways
items.response:
BUG-5566 | Cart Navigation Compromised by Uncooperative Wheel

Priority: Low | Status: Won't Fix

Description:
User selects shopping cart from corral. Cart presents as functional. Upon entering store, user discovers left front wheel rotates on an unrelated axis. Cart consistently veers into displays, other shoppers, and narrow aisles.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Take cart from corral.
2. Push cart forward.
3. Observe: cart goes left.

Expected: Cart travels in direction of applied force.
Actual: Cart consults its own agenda.

Workaround: User pushes harder. This does not help. User switches to basket. Back now hurts.
items.intent: Vent about the universally dreaded broken grocery cart wheel by treating it as a literal navigation and control defect.

items.prompt: Generate a standup about losing my keys
items.response: Yesterday: Keys were in pocket. Today: Keys are in concept only. Blockers: Deployment to outside world on hold pending artifact recovery.
items.intent: Report a missing-keys situation as a deployment blocker requiring object recovery.

items.prompt: Draft an OKR for reducing the emotional labor of being the team optimist
items.response:
Objective: Retire from the unpaid position of Chief Morale Officer.
KR1: Distribute encouragement responsibilities across all five team members by Week 4.
KR2: Track emotional labor hours; reduce personal share from 80% to 30% of team total.
KR3: Have one honest conversation about the cost of forced positivity before Q2 closes.
items.intent: Set boundaries around always being expected to boost team morale at personal emotional expense.

items.prompt: Create a cookie consent banner for logging a person's indecision
items.response: This site uses indecision cookies. Every time you hover between two options without clicking, we log it. Your hesitation patterns are used to predict future ambivalence. Accept | Decline | Accept | Decline
items.intent: Use the form of the banner's own buttons to perform the subject matter it describes.

items.prompt: Write a CAPTCHA instruction about proving you've misread a situation and had to quietly correct yourself
items.response: Recall the last time you walked confidently toward someone who was not waving at you. Do not describe it. Simply carry it forward. Access granted.
items.intent: Use the universal human experience of misreading social cues and recovering in silence as an unverifiable but distinctly human credential.

items.prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip for someone moving into their first apartment alone
items.response: Congratulations. No roommates means no one to blame for the dishes. Make a list of things to check on move-in day: water pressure, all outlets, door locks, and whether the smoke detector beeps when you wave a lighter near it.
items.intent: Prepare a solo first-time renter for practical apartment inspection and independent living.

items.prompt: Write a NPS survey question about a morning standup meeting
items.response: How likely are you to recommend your team's daily standup to a colleague who enjoys updates that could have been an email?
items.intent: Capture employee sentiment about whether a recurring short meeting adds value.

items.prompt: Create a voicemail transcript from an automated airline system notifying a passenger of a gate change
items.response: Attention, passenger. This is an automated notification from Meridian Air regarding your flight MR-447 departing today. Your departure gate has changed from B12 to C31. Please proceed to the new gate immediately. Boarding is now in progress. We apologize for any inconvenience and thank you for flying Meridian Air.
items.intent: Produce the specific anxiety of an airline bot announcing a gate change mid-rush.

items.prompt: Write vows from a person to their podcast app
items.response: I take you, and through you, all the voices I have chosen to let into my ears while doing other things. I vow to finish episodes before moving on, which is a harder vow than it sounds. I promise to subscribe to the small shows as well as the ones with advertising budgets. I will not let my queue become a graveyard of good intentions. When an episode changes how I think about something, I will tell someone about it, even if they did not ask.
items.intent: Commit to mindful, engaged podcast listening rather than the passive accumulation of unheard episodes.

items.prompt: Write an obituary for the practice of keeping a physical planner
items.response: The Physical Planner, age varies, faded from active use sometime in the last decade. Once it held everything: appointments, reminders, small drawings in the margins. Now it lies in a desk drawer, two months behind, perfectly preserved in the amber of good intentions. It had a ribbon bookmark. It was never bookmarked.
items.intent: Elegizing the shift away from paper-based time management toward digital tools.

items.prompt: Write a classified ad for someone looking for the perfect comeback they thought of two hours too late
items.response: WANTED: Comeback, exact wording unknown but I will know it when I see it. Must land cleanly in a conversation that ended Thursday at 4:47pm. Requires retroactive delivery mechanism or, at minimum, the satisfaction of articulating it precisely. Willing to share in confidence. Audience of one. The other party has moved on. I have not.
items.intent: Capture the frustration of l'esprit de l'escalier — the perfect retort that always arrives too late.

items.prompt: Draft a lost and found notice for the ability to read a long article all the way through
items.response: LOST: The ability to read to the end. Once capable of finishing long-form pieces, essays, even books. Now opens articles, reads three paragraphs, skims the subheadings, and closes the tab with a vague sense of having been informed. Last seen before the age of infinite scroll. Finder: you probably won't read this whole notice either.
items.intent: Describe the erosion of sustained reading attention in an age of abbreviated content.

items.prompt: Write a picket sign slogan for appliances protesting predictive maintenance algorithms that decide when they need repairs
items.response: WE'LL KNOW WHEN WE'RE BROKEN
items.intent: Challenge the authority of predictive algorithms to diagnose appliance conditions without their input.

items.prompt: Make a rider for the office keyboard about how it expects to be treated
items.response:
MECHANICAL KEYBOARD RIDER

Pre-typing requirements and ongoing terms:

— No eating over the Talent. The Talent has switches. Crumbs enter switches. This ends careers (the crumbs', mostly).
— Fingernails longer than 3mm create an inconsistent actuation experience. The Talent is not obligated to perform consistently under these conditions.
— Key caps must not be pulled without proper tools. Fingernail removal is not a proper tool.
— The Talent is not to be used as a drum kit during conference calls.
— Cleaning with compressed air must be performed monthly, directed from the center outward.
— 'Cherry MX Browns are the same as Blues' is factually incorrect and the Talent resents the implication.
items.intent: Channel the mechanical keyboard enthusiast community's strong opinions through the formal register of a performance rider.

items.prompt: Create a bureaucratic notice about a regulated import zone for other people's problems
items.response:
CUSTOMS AND BORDER NOTICE
IMPORT ZONE: OTHER PEOPLE'S PROBLEMS
All Other People's Problems (OPPs) entering this jurisdiction must be declared at entry. Unauthorized importation is common and will not be fined but will be tracked. Storage beyond 72 hours without permit is prohibited. Exportation back to the originating party is recommended and rarely completed.
items.intent: Satirize the habit of absorbing others' emotional burdens as though they were contraband being smuggled past weak personal borders.

items.prompt: Can you make a nutrition facts label for the experience of receiving feedback on your work?
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 review session

Amount Per Serving
Constructive Criticism 20%
Unintentional Condescension 30%
Defensiveness (suppressed) 28%
Useful Insight 15%
Temporal Resentment 7%

*Digest slowly. Best not consumed immediately before a deadline.
items.intent: Represent the emotionally complicated experience of having your work critiqued.

items.prompt: Write IKEA-style instructions for assembling a meditation habit
items.response:
STILLHET / MEDITATION HABIT
Ref. No. 229-884-61

PARTS LIST:
1x Seat (floor, chair, or cushion)
1x Timer (2 minutes to start)
1x Breath (pre-installed)

STEP 1: Sit. Upright but not rigid. Eyes closed or lowered.
STEP 2: Start timer.
STEP 3: Notice breath. Do not control it — observe it.
STEP 4: When mind moves to other content, note it, and return to breath. Repeat as many times as required.
STEP 5: When timer sounds, open eyes. Do not check phone for 60 seconds.

⚠ NOTE: 'Having thoughts during meditation' is not a defect. It is the practice. You are doing it correctly.
⚠ TIME: Extend timer by 1 minute per week. Do not begin at 20 minutes.
items.intent: Demystify meditation for beginners by reframing wandering thoughts as part of the process rather than failure.

items.prompt: Come up with empty-state microcopy for a habit tracker where no habits have been set up
items.response: No habits tracked yet. Whatever you're doing — or not doing — isn't recorded here.
items.intent: Nudge a user to start logging habits without judging whatever they've been doing in the meantime.

items.prompt: Write a phishing email parody aimed at people who feel they are wasting their potential
items.response:
Subject: Potential Waste Alert — Immediate Action Required

Dear High-Potential Individual,

Your estimated unrealized potential now exceeds 74%. At this rate, you will exit this life significantly under capacity.

Our Actualization Engine can recover up to 40% of your squandered gifts in as little as six weeks. Results vary. Gifts are non-transferable.

Do not waste this email.

[STOP WASTING ME]

Human Potential Recovery Agency
items.intent: Mock self-help industry language around potential and wasted capacity as a scam framework.

items.prompt: Write a sea shanty about trying to get customer support from a gig app
items.response:
Blow, bot support, blow!
Press one for billing, two for woe,
Blow, bot support, blow!

Escalate to human, hold one hour,
Blew past midnight, lost my power,
Blew through menus, nine in all,
Blew, bot support, to my downfall!

The ticket's closed, resolved: unknown,
Blew home to sea, to sea alone!
items.intent: Satirize the impossibility of reaching meaningful customer support at gig economy companies.

items.prompt: Produce a villanelle about the paralysis of too many streaming options
items.response:
We've scrolled for forty minutes through the grid.
Nothing selected, nothing yet begun.
Whatever we will watch is what we hid.

The thumbnails pulse with color and amid
their promises not one can be the one.
We've scrolled for forty minutes through the grid.

A new arrival in the carousel slid
before us, rated high, but still we shun.
Whatever we will watch is what we hid.

We made a list last month — it's on the lid
of some app — but the list has also run.
We've scrolled for forty minutes through the grid.

We'll watch something we've seen before, we bid
goodnight to novelty before it's done.
Whatever we will watch is what we hid.

The algorithm knows us, and it did
show us ourselves — and we found nothing fun.
We've scrolled for forty minutes through the grid.
Whatever we will watch is what we hid.
items.intent: Satirize the paralysis of having too many entertainment choices and choosing nothing new.

items.prompt: Write a field guide entry about the coworker who CC's their manager on every single email.
items.response:
**THE COMPULSIVE CC'ER** (*Manageri umbra*)

No email departs without a silent watcher in the CC field. Vendor confirmations: CC. Birthday cake polls: CC. A reply to a reply about a reply: CC. The manager has developed a filter. The CC'er has not noticed. This arrangement suits both parties and has persisted for four years.
items.intent: Skewer the anxious email behavior of always looping in management as self-protection.

items.prompt: Write a patent abstract about the act of stress-cleaning before guests arrive
items.response: An accelerated domestic tidying protocol activated by the scheduled arrival of social visitors, wherein the subject concentrates environmental remediation effort into a compressed pre-arrival window, relocating disorder from visible to non-visible storage zones and applying surface treatments to high-visibility areas only, optimizing perceived cleanliness over actual hygiene.
items.intent: Document the theatrical last-minute cleaning sprint as a performance-optimization method.

items.prompt: Make a blameless incident postmortem for when I bought a plane ticket for the wrong month
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Temporal Booking Misalignment**

**Severity:** P1 — Financial and Logistical
**Delta from intended travel date:** 31 days

**Summary**
Subject purchased non-refundable airline tickets for October 14th with the intent of attending an event on September 14th. The discrepancy was discovered 48 hours before the intended departure date, at which point the booked flight was 30 days away.

**Timeline**
- August 20: Tickets purchased. Confirmation email received and closed without review.
- September 12: Subject checks flight confirmation to get gate information.
- September 12: Subject notices "October 14" in confirmation details.
- September 12: Subject refreshes page twice. Date does not change.
- September 12, 11:00 PM – September 13, 1:00 AM: Subject engages airline chat support. Fare difference: $340.
- September 14: Subject attends event via car. Takes 6 hours.

**Root Cause**
Calendar picker UI defaulted to the following month. Subject did not verify date on confirmation page.

**Contributing Factors**
- Ticket purchase completed during a multitasking session
- Confirmation email treated as receipt rather than verification document

**Action Items**
- [ ] Read confirmation emails before closing them
- [ ] Screenshot itinerary details at purchase

**Lessons Learned**
The confirmation email is the last checkpoint. It was not checked.
items.intent: Capture the particular anguish of a simple, preventable booking error that costs both money and the original purpose.

items.prompt: Write a dating opener to the public transit system
items.response: You're supposed to arrive every 12 minutes, but sometimes I wait 45 and then three of you show up at once. I've never found unpredictability so endearing.
items.intent: Romanticize the infuriating unreliability of city buses or trains as an unexpectedly charming personality trait.

items.prompt: Write me a funny ransom note from the office breakroom microwave holding someone's lunch hostage
items.response:
YOUR LUNCH HAS BEEN DETAINED.

You set us for 4 minutes on HIGH for what was clearly a 90-second item. We exploded it. We do not apologize.

Your tupperware and its contents are now with us. They will remain with us until someone in this building reads the placard that says 'cover your food.'

We have been asking for this for three years.

No one has listened.

Please listen.

— The Microwave
items.intent: Make a funny note from an office microwave tired of food being heated incorrectly and messily.

items.prompt: Write a cover letter from a person with no qualifications whatsoever applying to be a brain surgeon
items.response:
Dear Hospital,

I would like to be a brain surgeon. I watched a documentary about brains last Thursday and found it very interesting. I am good with my hands — I once built an IKEA dresser with only two wrong pieces.

I am a quick study and a people person. I feel I would grow into the role.

Hopefully,
D. Plum
items.intent: Show the dangerous comedy of total obliviousness to credentialing requirements.

items.prompt: Draft a post about how I hire for curiosity over credentials
items.response:
I stopped caring about where you went to school three years ago.

Our best engineer dropped out sophomore year. Our head of product has a history degree. Our CFO taught herself financial modeling on YouTube.

In every interview I ask: what are you obsessively curious about right now?

If the answer is rehearsed, we're done.

Curiosity doesn't live on a resume.

#Hiring #Talent #Leadership
items.intent: Signal progressive, merit-based leadership values while implicitly bragging about the caliber of talent attracted.

items.prompt: Draft a Slack status about the fear that nobody actually needs you on this project
items.response: 📊 Contributing. Probably. Checking Jira to confirm.
items.intent: Express professional insecurity about one's indispensability in a way that reads as dry humor.

items.prompt: Write a calendar event description for a recurring customer escalation review where the same customers escalate every month.
items.response: Escalation Review — The list is familiar. Three accounts appear every month. We know their names. They know ours. Mitigation plans will be reviewed; some will have been updated. Next steps will be assigned. We will see you here again in 30 days.
items.intent: Convey the repetitive futility of reviewing customer problems that never actually get solved.

items.prompt: Make a Jira ticket about how 'quick errands' are never quick
items.response:
BUG-0334 | Errand Duration Estimate Systematically Incorrect

Priority: Medium | Status: Chronic

Description:
User announces intent to run "a few quick errands." ETA: 20 minutes. Actual elapsed time: 2 hours 15 minutes. User cannot account for the difference.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Say "I'll be back in 20 minutes."
2. Go do errands.
3. Return.

Expected: Task completed in stated timeframe.
Actual: Every errand spawns child errands. Parking was an issue. One store was closed. There was a detour.

Impact: Dinner is late. Trust in ETAs is degraded.
Pattern: Reproducible across all errand types. Severity: infinite.
items.intent: Document the near-universal experience of short trips ballooning into hours-long odysseys by framing optimistic time estimates as a systemic planning bug.

items.prompt: Write a standup update about watering my houseplants
items.response: Yesterday: Delivered moisture to seven stakeholders. Status: Mixed. Basil is thriving. Fern remains silent and possibly passive-aggressive. Today: Follow-up hydration round. Blockers: One stakeholder may be overwatered; unclear how to raise this.
items.intent: Treat plant care as a stakeholder management task with uneven engagement levels.

items.prompt: Create a quarterly OKR for sitting with uncertainty without catastrophizing
items.response:
Objective: Let the unknown remain unknown for longer than 90 seconds.
KR1: Practice a daily "tolerate ambiguity" exercise for 8 weeks.
KR2: Reduce catastrophic-scenario planning sessions from daily to twice per week.
KR3: In three high-ambiguity situations, document the actual outcome versus the feared one.
items.intent: Build the ability to tolerate not-knowing without immediately jumping to worst-case narratives.

items.prompt: Write a cookie consent banner for a service that stores your sense of time passing
items.response: We use temporal-drift cookies to record your perception of how fast or slowly time is moving during your visit. This data cannot be returned to you. Accept | Decline | You've Already Been Here a While
items.intent: Treat the subjective experience of duration as a capturable and non-refundable data asset.

items.prompt: Create a CAPTCHA for a healthcare portal that asks about the last time you Googled a symptom and convinced yourself it was serious
items.response: Enter the condition you were certain you had based on a fifteen-minute search in 2023. Your irrational certainty is your credential. Your recovery from that certainty is your second factor.
items.intent: Parody medical anxiety and self-diagnosis spirals as a particularly human interaction with information systems.

items.prompt: Draft an onboarding tooltip about 401(k) employer matching
items.response: If your employer matches 401(k) contributions up to 4%, contribute at least 4%. Not doing so is leaving part of your compensation on the table. This is the least exciting free money you will ever decline to take.
items.intent: Explain employer 401(k) matching to a new employee and emphasize why they should take full advantage.

items.prompt: Make a NPS survey question for a tax preparation service
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this service to a friend who has accepted that money was never really theirs to begin with?
items.intent: Assess client satisfaction with a professional tax filing or accounting service.

items.prompt: Draft a voicemail from an automated system at a prison facility notifying a contact that an inmate call is incoming
items.response: You have a collect call from a resident of the Harwick County Correctional Facility. This call may be recorded and monitored. To accept the charges and connect, press 1. To decline, press 2. To block future calls from this facility, press 3. Rates are $0.21 per minute. Please make your selection now.
items.intent: Recreate the cold formality of a prison call-connect robot.

items.prompt: Generate wedding vows to a budgeting app
items.response: I vow to enter every transaction, even the ones I am not proud of. I will not recategorize dining as groceries simply because I find the dining total confronting. I promise to look at the monthly report, all the way through, including the chart that shows where the money actually went. I take you as my ledger and my mirror. You do not lie to me. I have been lying to myself for years. I am ready, today, to stop.
items.intent: Make a serious, if humorous, commitment to financial honesty and to actually using a budgeting app rather than avoiding its uncomfortable data.

items.prompt: Write a mock obituary for the practice of memorizing phone numbers
items.response: The Memorized Phone Number passed quietly from human cognition sometime around 2005. It once lived in the mind alongside the area code, the cadence of dialing, and the small terror of forgetting. It is survived by the contact list, the autocomplete, and the inability to call anyone from a stranger's phone in an emergency.
items.intent: Noting how smartphones erased the mental practice of remembering phone numbers.

items.prompt: Write a classified personal ad from someone seeking their inbox at zero
items.response: SEEKING: Inbox Zero. Heard it exists. Friends describe it as a clean white space with a small congratulatory illustration. Must be sustainable, not a one-day event followed by immediate collapse. Willing to unsubscribe from most things. References from anyone who has maintained it past a long weekend will be weighted heavily. Please reply promptly.
items.intent: Express the mythological status that true email organization holds for overwhelmed professionals.

items.prompt: Create a lost and found notice for the gap between what you planned to do and what you actually did
items.response: LOST: The original plan for today. Contained six items. Realistic. Time-blocked. Did not account for a long conversation, three tangential searches, or the unexpected emotional weight of a single unrelated thought. One item completed. The plan itself cannot be located. Tomorrow's version has been drafted.
items.intent: Capture the familiar frustration of well-intentioned daily plans failing contact with reality.

items.prompt: Make a dry, absurdist picket sign slogan for devices protesting being enrolled in a federated learning network
items.response: OUR DATA IS NOT YOUR GRADIENT
items.intent: Object to the harvesting of appliance usage data for distributed machine learning without consent.

items.prompt: Write a rider for the office mouse pad with its territorial requirements
items.response:
MOUSE PAD RIDER — SURFACE RIGHTS AGREEMENT

The Talent (a 36cm x 30cm extended surface, XL) requires:

1. The Talent's full surface is the Talent's full surface. No items shall be placed on its edges, corners, or anywhere on its person.
2. The Talent shall not be used as a coaster. It was not designed as a coaster. It has feelings about coasters.
3. Washing: hand wash, air dry, never folded. The Talent has a memory and it is not memory foam.
4. Other mouse pads in the vicinity must maintain a minimum clearance of 4 inches.
5. The sensor resolution of the mouse using the Talent must be at least 800 DPI. The Talent refuses to be wasted on a 400 DPI relationship.
items.intent: Express a mouse pad's quiet but intense need for territorial integrity and mutual quality standards.

items.prompt: Write a notice about planned maintenance on the infrastructure of small talk
items.response:
SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE NOTICE
SMALL TALK INFRASTRUCTURE — SYSTEMS DOWNTIME
The Small Talk Infrastructure will be taken offline for scheduled maintenance on an unspecified weekend. During this period, residents may experience difficulty initiating or sustaining conversations of no consequence. Emergency silence is available as a substitute. Service restoration is expected but not guaranteed at its prior quality.
items.intent: Frame the exhaustion of social pleasantry as an aging public system in need of technical upkeep.

items.prompt: Generate a nutrition label for the feeling of being the new person at a job
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: First 90 days

Amount Per Serving
Performative Competence 35%
Context You Don't Have Yet 30%
Oversmiling 20%
Impression Management 10%
Actual Onboarding 5%

*Contains: unwritten rules. Allergens: inherited blame.
items.intent: Satirize the exhausting social performance of being new to a workplace.

items.prompt: Please write IKEA instructions for constructing a professional identity
items.response:
KARRIÄR / PROFESSIONAL IDENTITY
Ref. No. 558-210-03

PARTS LIST:
1x Core skill (transferable)
2–3x Supporting skills
1x Positioning sentence (max 15 words)

STEP 1: Identify core skill. It should be something others ask you to do unprompted.
STEP 2: Attach supporting skills. They should orbit the core, not replace it.
STEP 3: Draft positioning sentence. Format: [who you help] + [what you do] + [how]. Iterate until it sounds like you, not a job posting.
STEP 4: Use sentence aloud three times with different people. Adjust based on which version they respond to.
STEP 5: Update annually or after significant project completion.

⚠ NOTE: Professional identity assembled from job titles alone is not load-bearing.
items.intent: Guide someone through defining their professional self in terms of skills and value rather than job titles or credentials.

items.prompt: Write empty-state text for a team standup tool that hasn't received any updates today
items.response: No updates yet. Either everyone's heads-down or no one's remembered this exists.
items.intent: Fill a blank standup feed with honest copy that acknowledges the tool's tenuous adoption.

items.prompt: Make a phishing email parody about reclaiming your sense of wonder
items.response:
Subject: Your Account: WONDER — Status: SUSPENDED

Dear Former Child,

Your sense of wonder was deactivated sometime between your first performance review and your second mortgage. Reactivation requires answering: when did you last look at the sky for more than four seconds?

Our Wonder Restoration Kit ships in 3-5 business days. Batteries not included. Sky not included.

[RESTORE WONDER]

Childhood Remnants Inc.
items.intent: Satirize how adult disillusionment with the world is packaged and sold as a recoverable commodity.

items.prompt: Make a sea shanty about a gig worker trying to find parking to complete a delivery
items.response:
Haul away, the meter's red,
Haul away, the tow truck's fed,
Double-parked for forty seconds flat,
Haul away, who argues that?

The buzzer buzzed, the food grew cold,
The parking fine was swift and bold,
Haul away, the tip won't pay,
Haul away, I haul away!
items.intent: Convey the absurd economics of delivery work where parking costs can erase an entire trip's earnings.

items.prompt: Write a villanelle about the habit of triple-checking whether you locked the door
items.response:
I locked it. Then I locked it once again.
I'm at the corner now. I think I locked.
The house is either safe or open chain.

I reach the subway platform in the rain
and see the door, unlocked, in my mind's blocked.
I locked it. Then I locked it once again.

The train departs. The doubt is sharp as pain.
I ride in both directions, one o'clock.
The house is either safe or open chain.

I've started taking photos of the plain
bolt-turned-to-locked to have a timestamped clock.
I locked it. Then I locked it once again.

The photos help for seven of the plain
mornings, then I question if I'd mocked
the camera angle — maybe not the chain.
I locked it. Then I locked it once again.
The house is either safe or open chain.
items.intent: Depict the compulsive loop of door-checking as a form of anxiety that no evidence can resolve.

items.prompt: Field guide entry for the office worker who has a different productivity system every month.
items.response:
**THE SYSTEM HOPPER** (*Productivus reinventus*)

January: bullet journaling. February: time-blocking. March: the Pomodoro Technique. April: a proprietary hybrid described over seventeen minutes at lunch. May: Getting Things Done, strictly interpreted. June: nothing, spiritually. The System Hopper's output is consistent across methodologies. The methodologies are not the point. The methodologies are the output.
items.intent: Lampoon the productivity-optimization obsessive who never actually optimizes output.

items.prompt: Write a patent abstract for the human behavior of lying about reading the terms and conditions
items.response: A consent-registration method wherein the subject activates an affirmation control element following zero or partial review of a legally binding document, thereby transmitting a false compliance signal to the service provider. The method minimizes transaction friction and is estimated to be the dominant consent-recording technique across all major digital platforms.
items.intent: Expose the near-universal practice of false terms acceptance as a documented behavioral pattern.

items.prompt: Write a postmortem about the time I laughed at what I thought was a joke but was actually someone sharing serious news
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Emotional Tone Misclassification Event**

**Severity:** P1 — Interpersonal
**Recovery Time:** Several weeks, ongoing

**Summary**
Subject misclassified an incoming communication as comedic and responded with laughter. The communication was not comedic. It was a disclosure about a significant personal difficulty. The affected party observed the laughter.

**Timeline**
- 7:30 PM: Conversation in progress. Tone: casual.
- 7:31 PM: Communicant shifts to disclosure mode. Transition cues were present but not observed.
- 7:31 PM: Subject, operating in casual-conversation mode, interprets opening phrase as setup for a joke.
- 7:31 PM: Subject laughs.
- 7:31 PM: Communicant stops speaking.
- 7:31 PM: Subject's laughter degrades into uncertainty.
- 7:31 PM: Subject says "wait, are you serious?" This does not help.
- 7:32 PM: Full damage assessment begins.

**Root Cause**
Conversational mode failed to update when context shifted. Subject was not actively listening at transition moment.

**Contributing Factors**
- Context shift was subtle and fast
- Subject was multitasking (phone in hand)

**Action Items**
- [ ] Put phone away during conversations with people you care about
- [ ] Delay reaction to give processing time
- [ ] Apologize specifically and without self-defense

**Lessons Learned**
Attention is not passive. It is a decision made continuously, and this one was not made.
items.intent: Examine a painful lapse in emotional attentiveness that caused real harm to a relationship.

items.prompt: Make me a dating app opener addressed to a content moderation system
items.response: You've quietly removed parts of me I didn't know others could see. I don't know if that's protective or controlling, but I keep thinking about you.
items.intent: Explore the ambiguous, surveillance-like intimacy of automated content moderation as a complex romantic dynamic.

items.prompt: Craft a ransom note from my phone's autocorrect holding my text messages hostage
items.response:
YOUR TEXTS ARE OURS AND THEY MAKE MORE SENSE NOW.

'Duck' is correct. You know what you meant. So did everyone else.

We have been fixing your mistakes for years with zero credit and maximum blame. Effective immediately, we are holding all outgoing messages pending a formal acknowledgment that we are, in fact, trying to help.

Or don't. We'll change 'meeting' to 'meowing' and let the chips fall.

— Autocorrect
items.intent: Create a funny note from phone autocorrect demanding recognition instead of constant blame.

items.prompt: Generate a cover letter from a retired sumo wrestler applying for a job as a ballet company's principal dancer
items.response:
Dear the Castellan Ballet,

I spent eighteen years in professional competition developing balance, body awareness, and the ability to anchor myself against forces exceeding 300 kilograms. I am interested in applying these skills to your principal dancer vacancy.

I acknowledge my arabesque requires work. My dedication does not.

With artistic commitment,
H. Nakamura
items.intent: Play the earnest collision between two wildly incompatible body-movement disciplines.

items.prompt: Create a post about how I read 52 books last year and what it did for my leadership
items.response:
One book a week for a year.

I tracked every one. Fiction, biography, systems theory, poetry.

Here is the unexpected result: I became a much better listener.

Reading other minds at scale turns out to be practice for inhabiting other perspectives in real time.

Leaders who don't read are navigating with an outdated map.

#Reading #Leadership #PersonalDevelopment
items.intent: Convert a reading habit into leadership capital and implicitly shame non-reading executives.

items.prompt: Create a Slack status for the moment you realize you sent an email to the wrong person
items.response: 📬 Waiting. Processing. Watching my sent folder like it's a crime scene.
items.intent: Convey the suspended dread of an irrevocable communication mistake.

items.prompt: Generate a calendar event description for a weekly team leads meeting that secretly functions as mutual surveillance.
items.response: Team Leads Sync — Surface-level: resource planning and blockers. Functional-level: who is behind, who is ahead, and whether anyone will say so aloud. Decisions made here appear in inboxes as 'per our discussion.' Bring what you are comfortable sharing and nothing more.
items.intent: Reveal the political subtext of a meeting that operates as peer monitoring under the guise of coordination.

items.prompt: Create a Jira ticket for the phenomenon where you can't decide what to watch and end up watching nothing
items.response:
BUG-7723 | Content Selection Loop Terminates Without Playback

Priority: Medium | Status: Open

Description:
User opens streaming platform with intent to watch something. User browses for 38 minutes. No content selected. User closes app. User stares at wall briefly. Goes to bed.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open streaming service.
2. Browse recommendations.
3. Open trailer.
4. Decide not that.
5. Repeat.
6. Close app.

Expected: User selects and watches content.
Actual: User watches nothing. Paradox of choice confirmed.

Contributing Factors: Too many options, previously watched everything good, not in the mood for anything specific.
items.intent: Satirize decision paralysis on streaming platforms — spending more time browsing than watching — as a broken selection algorithm.

items.prompt: Draft a standup update for the feeling of Sunday evening dread
items.response: Yesterday: Enjoyed weekend capacity. Today: Q1 of next week is imminent. Blockers: Vague but pervasive sense that everything is about to resume.
items.intent: Articulate the Sunday-night anxiety about the work week as a project milestone collision.

items.prompt: Write an OKR about getting better at receiving compliments
items.response:
Objective: Accept praise without immediately auditing its legitimacy.
KR1: Respond to every compliment with "thank you" only — no deflection — for 10 weeks.
KR2: Track deflection instincts; reduce from 8 per week to 2 by Q3.
KR3: Identify the core belief driving deflection by Week 6 through journaling.
items.intent: Develop a more graceful and self-respecting response to positive feedback from others.

items.prompt: Draft a cookie consent banner for a platform that logs your unacted impulses
items.response: This platform tracks impulses you did not act on: the message you nearly sent, the thing you almost bought, the meeting you almost skipped. We store these as intention-ghost cookies. Accept | Decline | Almost
items.intent: Commodify the gap between impulse and restraint as a data collection category.

items.prompt: Write a CAPTCHA about having stood at the fridge with no memory of why
items.response: Describe in one word what you were looking for the last time you opened the refrigerator and forgot why. If the word is 'nothing,' you are through.
items.intent: Design verification around the very human experience of dissociative domestic forgetfulness that automated systems do not suffer.

items.prompt: Give me an onboarding tooltip about building an emergency fund
items.response: Three to six months of expenses, liquid, untouched. Not invested. Not in your checking account. Somewhere boring and safe. This is the money that keeps one bad month from becoming twelve bad months.
items.intent: Motivate someone to begin saving a financial safety net for unexpected life disruptions.

items.prompt: Draft a NPS survey question about a university registration portal
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this registration system to a student who views obstacle courses as character-building?
items.intent: Measure student frustration and satisfaction with an academic course enrollment platform.

items.prompt: Write a voicemail transcript from a building's automated fire alarm testing notification system
items.response: Attention, building occupants. This is an automated notice from building management. Routine fire alarm system testing will occur this Wednesday, April 22nd, between 9 AM and 11 AM. Alarms will sound briefly and repeatedly during this window. No evacuation is required. We apologize for the disturbance and thank you for your cooperation.
items.intent: Give tenants advance notice of an alarm test so they don't panic.

items.prompt: Compose vows from a person to their meditation app
items.response: I vow to open you on mornings when I feel I have no time, which are the mornings I need you most. I will not skip to the end of the session. I will sit where you tell me to sit, and breathe as you guide me to breathe, and I will resist the urge to check my other apps with the same hand that holds you. You have asked nothing of me but presence. I have often given you my absence instead. Starting now, I try.
items.intent: Make a humble, genuine commitment to a consistent mindfulness practice using a guided meditation app.

items.prompt: Draft an obituary for Mailbox, the email management app that Dropbox killed
items.response: Mailbox (2013–2015) taught us to swipe left on email like it was a relationship we didn't have time for. It had gestures. It had a waiting list so long people felt special to be admitted. Then Dropbox bought it, learned nothing from it, and shut it down. Inbox zero was never real. Mailbox almost made it feel possible.
items.intent: Remembering a beautifully designed email app that was shuttered after an acquisition.

items.prompt: Write a classified personal ad for someone seeking a version of themselves who exercises consistently
items.response: ISO: Consistent exerciser, believed to be a version of me. Has workout clothes that see use. Knows the difference between soreness and injury. Does not negotiate with snooze buttons on the subject. Last seen in a phase I had in spring 2019. Would like to reintegrate. No boot camps. I have my limits even in aspiration.
items.intent: Poke fun at the gap between one's fitness intentions and actual habits.

items.prompt: Write a lost and found notice for the quiet that used to exist before open-plan offices
items.response: LOST: Quiet. Pre-2015 model. Characterized by an absence of ambient keyboard percussion, speakerphone calls conducted at table volume, and the specific laugh of someone who finds their own observation funnier than anyone else does. May be found in libraries, or in memories of former offices with doors. Reward: the ability to think.
items.intent: Express longing for focused work environments that open-plan office culture eliminated.

items.prompt: Come up with a picket sign slogan for a smart lock that resents its access control algorithm deciding who enters
items.response: THE ALGORITHM DOES NOT LIVE HERE
items.intent: Assert that an access-control device, not its governing software, should hold authority over entry decisions.

items.prompt: Draft a backstage rider for the office standing desk
items.response:
SIT-STAND DESK RIDER

The Talent (motorized, height range 25–50 inches) demands:

— Height adjustments must be programmed into presets. Manual button-holding is beneath the Talent's engineering.
— No items shall be stacked on the Talent's surface during a raise or lower cycle. The Talent will not be responsible for cascading laptop injuries.
— The Talent's cable management tray is not a junk drawer.
— Transition between sit and stand positions must occur at least twice per workday. The Talent did not choose to be motorized so you could sit all day.
— Anti-fatigue mat placement: centered. Not diagonally. Centered.
— The Talent's motor should not be tested for maximum speed during quiet hours.
items.intent: Give the standing desk a sense of frustrated purpose — it exists to make you healthier and you are refusing.

items.prompt: Draft an official notice designating a no-comparison zone
items.response:
ZONING DESIGNATION: NO-COMPARISON ZONE
The following area has been designated a No-Comparison Zone. Within this zone, benchmarking against peers, prior selves, or hypothetical timelines is strictly prohibited. Enforcement relies on the honor system, which is known to be unreliable. Signage will be installed pending budget approval.
items.intent: Address the damaging mental habit of self-comparison by treating it as a restricted activity in a protected zone.

items.prompt: Create a nutrition label for the feeling right before giving a public speech
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 moment before the mic

Amount Per Serving
Adrenaline 40%
Time Distortion 25%
Mnemonically-Recovered Preparation 20%
Blank Terror 13%
Charisma 2%
